key_insight: Protecting knowledge in LLM continual learning works better in sparse activation feature space than in raw weight space, because SAE features isolate task-relevant information more cleanly.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a memory-efficient, no-replay alternative to weight regularization for continual adaptation of large language models while reducing catastrophic forgetting.
method: Use pretrained sparse autoencoders (SAEs) to build a monosemantic feature dictionary.
Construct a compact task-specific SAE feature mask from current-task data only.
Regularize activations in feature space to balance stability and plasticity, with EWC emerging as a special weight-space case.
evidence_signal: On TRACE and MedCL continual learning benchmarks, the method outperforms traditional weight-space regularization such as EWC and is strongest among non-task-specific architectures.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and availability of pretrained SAEs and on how well the feature mask captures task-relevant concepts.
paper_title: From Weights to Features: SAE-Guided Activation Regularization for LLM Continual Learning
